title: Limonium tetragonum
taxonomy: Limonium tetragonum is classified within the family Plumbaginaceae, commonly known as the leadwort family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships. Limonium tetragonum is further classified as a member of the genus Limonium, which comprises about 100 species of flowering plants. The genus Limonium is a part of the order Plumbaginales, along with other related plant families. Limonium tetragonum's classification is also supported by its unique morphological features, such as its square stems and white or pink flowers.
uses: Limonium tetragonum has been used in traditional medicine due to its purported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, it has been employed to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant is said to have a calming effect on the nervous system and may be used to alleviate stress and anxiety. Additionally, it is believed to possess immunomodulatory and antiseptic properties, which could potentially aid in wound healing and infection prevention. More research is necessary to fully understand the efficacy and potential side effects of using Limonium tetragonum for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Limonium tetragonum, also known as Sea Lavender, is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation. Its antiseptic properties may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also believed to have antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment of fungal infections. Limonium tetragonum has been used in herbal medicine to help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, such as congestion and coughs. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce pain and swelling associated with arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.

constituents: Limonium tetragonum, also known as sea lavender or statice, is a plant species that contains various active constituents. These constituents include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have been found to possess antioxidant properties. The plant also contains phenolic acids, like caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, Limonium tetragonum contains volatile oils, including limonene and beta-pinene, which may contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their effects on human health.

side_effects: Limonium tetragonum is not typically associated with any adverse side effects when used in its natural form, but it may cause allergic reactions in some individuals.
